articles.name: Announcing customer satisfaction reports in real-time monitoring
articles.title: Announcing customer satisfaction reports in real-time monitoring
articles.body:
We're excited to announce that customer satisfaction (CSAT) ratings are now available in the real-time monitoring ticket progress dashboard. This enhancement allows supervisors and team leads to monitor customer satisfaction performance alongside ticket workflows, enabling faster responses to customer sentiment and more informed team management decisions.

This announcement answers the following questions:

  • What's changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What's changing?

Previously, customer satisfaction data was only available through historical reporting in Explore, making it difficult to track CSAT performance in real-time alongside your team's active ticket workflow.

Now, the ticket progress dashboard includes a dedicated customer satisfaction tab that shows real-time and historical CSAT metrics across email and messaging channels. Additionally, CSAT metrics are integrated into the ticket status tab for immediate visibility.

Key new capabilities include:

Real-time CSAT metrics:

  • Satisfaction distribution per channel (good with or without comments, bad with or without comments)
  • Satisfaction score by channel (percentage of good tickets against all rated tickets)
  • Good and bad ticket counts by channel
  • Rated tickets percentage by channel

Historical CSAT trends:

  • Time-series charts showing satisfaction ratings over configurable time periods (last 15 minutes to 30 days)
  • Separate historical views for email and messaging channels

Enhanced filtering:

  • New satisfaction ratings filter available across all dashboard tabs
  • Filter by specific rating types (good with comment, good without comment, bad with comment, bad without comment)

Why is Zendesk making this change?

This enhancement provides supervisors with a comprehensive, real-time view of both operational performance and customer satisfaction in a single dashboard. By bringing CSAT data into real-time monitoring, teams can:

  • Respond faster to customer sentiment: Identify satisfaction trends as they happen rather than waiting for end-of-day reports
  • Make data-driven coaching decisions: Connect agent performance metrics with customer satisfaction outcomes in real-time
  • Improve team performance: Track the immediate impact of process changes or training initiatives on customer satisfaction
  • Streamline workflows: Eliminate the need to switch between multiple reporting tools to understand the complete picture of team performance

This change aligns with Zendesk's commitment to providing actionable, real-time insights that help teams deliver exceptional customer experiences.

What do I need to do?

No action is required. This change will be available to all Professional and Enterprise accounts that have enabled CSAT functionality.  

For more information, see Using the ticket progress real-time dashboard.

We value your feedback! Share your thoughts about this update in the Zendesk community forum or contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 12, 2026 - Social Messaging Channels | Multiple Pods - Issue with Social Messaging Channels
articles.title: Service Incident - June 12, 2026 - Social Messaging Channels | Multiple Pods - Issue with Social Messaging Channels
articles.body:
Summary

On June 12, 2026, between 13:33 UTC and 16:30 UTC, Social Messaging customers across multiple pods experiencing an outage with Meta channels including WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and Instagram Messenger. During the outage, users were unable to send or receive messages on these channels.

Timeline

June 12, 2026 14:24 UTC | 7:24 AM PT
Our engineers are investigating the issue impacting social messaging channels. At the moment this appears to be a widespread Meta outage, and we will provide further updates when we hear more information from Meta. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

June 12, 2026 14:56 UTC | 07:56 AM PT
We are still working with Meta on resolving the issue impacting Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram messaging. We will provide an update as soon as we have new information to share.

June 12, 2026 16:27 UTC | 9:27 AM PT
We unfortunately do not have any substantive updates to share from Meta while we monitor the situation impacting Meta social messaging channels. We will be sure to share any news we can as soon as it is available.

June 12, 2026 17:36 UTC | 10:36 AM PT
We are pleased to hear that Meta has resolved the issue that was impacting Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram social messaging channels. Our own monitoring shows full recovery. Thank you.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by an outage with a Social Messaging Channels provider.

Resolution

The issue was fixed by our partner Social Messaging Channels provider.

 

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-06-26
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-06-26
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Admin Center
  • Copilot
  • Voice
  • Knowledge
  • App Marketplace
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)
  • Products with no updates this week

Admin Center

New

  • The content of the email that gets sent when accessing your account when your SSO provider is down has been updated to be simpler, clearer, and more closely aligned with our content standards. 

Copilot

New:

  • Zendesk is making AI more accessible by bringing the Copilot add-ons’ intelligent triage classification to Suite and Support Professional plans and above. This feature uses machine learning to automatically analyze and classify incoming tickets, helping you understand your top ticket drivers without manual effort.
  • Auto assist will no longer suggest solving a ticket unless it is asked to do so in a procedure.

Voice

Fixed:

  • Fixed an issue where click-to-call from the Web Widget did not connect when Help Center authentication was enabled.

Knowledge

New:

  • You can now connect Amazon S3 to your Zendesk account to make the content available wherever external content is used. See Connecting Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source.

App Marketplace

New:
  • Ticket Done (Support) (paid)
    • Ticket Done brings AI assistance directly into your Zendesk ticket sidebar so agents spend less time searching and more time solving. Ticket Done reads each incoming ticket and instantly surfaces AI-generated responses sourced from your actual Zendesk Help Center articles. No generic AI. No hallucinations. Every suggestion is grounded in your documentation. Generate a customer-ready reply in seconds. Ticket Done reads the ticket, searches your knowledge base using semantic AI, and drafts a response your agent can review and send. Need internal guidance before responding? Get Help surfaces the right policies, procedures, or escalation paths — visible only to the agent, never the customer.
  • Fusion (Theme)
    • Fusion is a premium Zendesk Guide theme that combines modern design, powerful functionality, and seamless navigation to help customers find answers faster. Designed for businesses of all sizes, Fusion transforms your help center into a professional support hub that enhances customer satisfaction and reduces support tickets. With its clean layouts, advanced search experience, and highly customizable design, Fusion delivers the perfect balance of aesthetics and usability. 

Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)

New:

  • The forecast vs actual page (EAP) now has a more granular CSV export.

  • New forecast variance metric that compares the volume forecasted with the actual volume received.

    Details on both can be found in Understanding and using the forecast vs actual page (EAP)

  • The default time off report has a new billable hours metric. It reflects the net number of paid hours lost as a result of the time-off request (Billable Hours = Total time-off duration – Duration of unpaid activities (within the same scheduled window)).  
  • Time off reasons can now be configured to be used by specific teams. More details in this article for Time off reasons: team visibility setting. 

Fixed:

  • Audit log events related to changes in performance boards and the extension tracking settings page will now appear in the WFM audit logs page.
  • The Neural Prophet forecasting algorithm is no longer available. Scenarios that were using it will have it changed to the Prophet algorithm on the next forecast recalculation.

Products with no updates this week

  • AI agents
  • Support
  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Analytics
  • Zendesk QA

articles.name: Announcing outbound calling restrictions by agent group for Voice
articles.title: Announcing outbound calling restrictions by agent group for Voice
articles.body:
We're excited to announce that Voice customers can now restrict outbound calling to specific groups of agents. Previously, when outbound calling was enabled on a line, all Voice agents could make calls from the associated number. Now, admins can specify which groups of agents are permitted to make outbound calls.  
This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Admins now have the ability to specify which groups of agents can use a line for outbound calls. By default, all groups and agents can make calls on all lines, but admins can restrict this access.

When agents open the call console, they’ll have only the lines their groups are authorized to use in their list of outbound numbers. 

Info: This per-group restriction is in addition to the existing brand restrictions.
Info: Callbacks aren't affected by this restriction because callbacks are initiated by the system, not by agents.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

This is a long-awaited and much-requested feature, especially for customers with dozens or even hundreds of lines across multiple countries who want to avoid the cost of misplaced international calls.

What do I need to do?

If you don't want to restrict any of your agents from using phone numbers to make calls, no action is required. Otherwise, update your phone line settings in the Admin Center, see Managing phone line settings.

Your existing groups, configured for incoming routing, might not provide the level of control you need for outbound permissions. If that’s the case, create new groups specifically for outbound calls. For example, you can create a group called “Spanish Agents” and assign it to the Spanish line so only those agents can use it.

If you have feedback or questions about this announcement, visit our community forum, where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center
articles.title: Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center
articles.body:
Admins can access Amazon Connect and the AWS Console from Contact Center to manage telephony and infrastructure settings. You can also review your Amazon Connect instance details in the Contact Center admin interface.

Admins can access Amazon Connect and the AWS Console from Contact Center to manage telephony and infrastructure settings. You can also review your Amazon Connect instance details in the Contact Center admin interface.

Related article:

  • About AWS services in Contact Center

To access Amazon Connect and the AWS Console

  1. Click the Zendesk product menu in the top bar, then select Contact center.
  2. Under Overview, review your Amazon Connect instance details.
  3. Click Amazon Connect Dashboard or AWS Console to open the related AWS service:
    • Amazon Connect Dashboard: View real-time metrics, manage contact center operations, configure routing profiles, and build contact flows.
    • AWS Console: Manage cloud infrastructure, user permissions, and other technical configuration settings.
Note: If you can't access Amazon Connect or the AWS Console, contact your administrator.

articles.name: Configuring call monitoring in Contact Center
articles.title: Configuring call monitoring in Contact Center
articles.body:
Summary: ◀▼

You can set up call monitoring so selected agents can listen to active calls, join calls when needed, and use the right workflow settings when joining. This requires adding the right real-time monitoring permissions in Amazon Connect and configuring notes and resolution codes in the Contact Center app, which helps you control call barge-in and monitor calls more effectively.

Admins can configure call monitoring for Contact Center by setting permissions in Amazon Connect and configuring workflows in the Zendesk Contact Center app. Use this article if you want selected users to listen to active calls, join calls when needed, and configure workflows when joining calls.

Admins can configure call monitoring for Contact Center by setting permissions in Amazon Connect and configuring workflows in the Zendesk Contact Center app. Use this article if you want selected users to listen to active calls, join calls when needed, and configure workflows when joining calls.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Configuring access permissions for call monitoring
  • Configuring workflows when joining calls

Related article:

  • Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center

Configuring access permissions for call monitoring

Before users can monitor and join active calls, you must assign them an Amazon Connect security profile that includes the required real-time monitoring permissions.

To give agents access to call monitoring
  1. In Amazon Connect, click Users > Security profiles.
  2. Click Add new security profile.
  3. Enter a name for the profile.
  4. Under Analytics and Optimization, select Real-time metrics and Real-time contact monitoring.
  5. Click Save.
  6. Click Users, then add the security profile to the agents who will monitor or join calls.

Configuring workflows when joining calls

This is only applicable for the Zendesk Contact Center app, not the native experience.

To configure workflows when joining calls
  1. In Contact Center, click the Settings icon (), then click Workflows.
  2. Find the workflow, then click Edit workflow.
  3. Configure the Notes and Resolution Codes, as shown.

articles.name: Announcing out of adherence automations for Zendesk WFM
articles.title: Announcing out of adherence automations for Zendesk WFM
articles.body:
Zendesk is introducing out of adherence automations for Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM). Admins can now configure automated real-time notifications that alert managers and agents the moment an agent is working on a task that doesn't match their current schedule.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Starting June 30, WFM admins can configure out of adherence automations in WFM. When an agent is working on a task that doesn't match what's currently scheduled, the automation triggers a real-time in-product toast notification to the manager and/or the affected agent.

Admins can configure:

  • Who receives the notification: the manager, the agent, or both.
  • The conditions that trigger the automation: an agent working on an unscheduled task.

Notifications are designed to be real-time and targeted, so teams can identify and address schedule discrepancies immediately as they occur.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Keeping agents on schedule is critical to meeting service level targets, but supervisors can't manually monitor every agent's activity in real time. 

Without automated alerting, out of adherence situations often go unnoticed until they've already impacted coverage and performance metrics.

Out of adherence automations give WFM supervisors and managers the visibility they need to surface schedule deviations the moment they happen, enabling immediate course-correction rather than after-the-fact reporting. This improves schedule adherence and operational efficiency across the team.

What do I need to do?

Starting June 30, you can configure out of adherence automations in WFM. No sign-up is required. See About workforce management automations.

To set up the automation, go to WFM > Automations and use the new out of adherence run event, selecting who should be notified and under what conditions. See Creating WFM automations to manage agent activity.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Troubleshooting common issues with AI agents
articles.title: Troubleshooting common issues with AI agents
articles.body:
Use this article if your AI agent doesn't respond as expected, escalates too early, or behaves differently across channels or instances. It covers the most common configuration and setup issues, along with the first checks to make before you contact Zendesk Customer Support.

Preliminary AI agent troubleshooting questions

  1. Is the AI agent enabled and assigned to the right channel? see Creating an AI agent to automatically resolve customer issues.
  2. Are relevant articles, knowledge sources, or integrations published and available to your customers? see Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents and Optimizing your knowledge content for generative AI.
  3. Is the correct language activated and supported?
  4. Have you reviewed the conversation logs for errors, escalation, or no response?
    5. Note: When you answer those questions, you know whether you face a configuration issue, a content issue, a channel, or a language issue, or something that needs Zendesk Customer Support.

When you follow the advice above, review common issues below:

The AI agent doesn’t respond to questions

Common causes to a lack of replies from your AI agent

  • The agent isn’t connected to the channel
  • Articles or sources are unpublished or inaccessible
  • The language isn't active
  • A setup or integration step is incomplete

To resolve this issue:

  1. Confirm the agent is enabled, assigned to the correct channel, and connected to the right knowledge source. For more information, see Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents and Reviewing conversation logs for AI agents.
  2. Check whether the language is active and supported

AI agent replies when it should hand off

Common causes

  • The fallback logic is too permissive
  • The procedure routes all ambiguous cases through the AI response step
  • Knowledge content is too broad to trigger a clean handoff

To resolve this issue:

  1. Review your escalation strategies and flows
  2. Verify your fallback behavior, and confidence or routing logic
  3. Test with weak-match examples
AI agent escalates too early or shows a technical error

Common causes

  • Inputs are missing or malformed
  • Flow logic doesn’t handle edge cases well
  • Integration errors or parameter mismatches
  • An unhandled condition in the procedure

To resolve this issue:

  1. Check your required parameters and fallback handling
  2. Test the integration in the integration builder and check that the request parameters match
  3. Check the HTTP status code
  4. Investigate any technical errors in the dialogue
  5. Verify the conversation logs for differences between successful and failed cases

For more information, see Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents.

Knowledge sources are missing, incomplete, or inconsistent

Common causes

  • The help center is not active and published
  • Articles are unpublished or their Visible to field does not allow customers and the AI agent to access the content
  • The source was not imported correctly
  • Different instances are on different rollout states. The feature is gated or not yet available everywhere. Search rules are not logical, labels are missing, or incorrect operators are used.

To resolve this issue:

  1. If you use articles from the Zendesk help center, ensure that the help center is active and published
  2. Ensure that article settings are published and the Placement > Visible to field is set to Everyone
  3. Confirm the knowledge sources were added in the right place, imported successfully, and is accessible to the AI agent. Verify imported knowledge sources for AI agents.
  4. Verify if the crawl failed because it timed out, is missing pages, or the crawl includes the right pages but the wrong content. see Troubleshooting issues with web crawler imports for AI agents and Using a web crawler to index external content.
  5. Compare configuration search rules between instances. for AI agents Advanced only
  6. Check for restricted articles and where the end user does not have access
AI agent behaves differently by language

Common causes to multiple responses for each language

  • The language hasn’t been activated
  • The agent is falling back because it can’t detect or serve the target language
  • The content exists, but not in the expected language

To resolve this issue:

  1. Confirm that the target language is activated
  2. Language detection is configured
  3. Content exists in the correct language
AI agent integration is failing

Common causes to a failed app integration with AI agents

  • Invalid credentials
  • Unsupported payload format
  • Endpoint or mapping errors
  • External system timeout or failure

To resolve this issue:

  1. Check the connection
  2. Verify the authentication and conversation logs
  3. Test the integration in the integration builder
  4. Check that the request parameters match and the HTTP status code
  5. Investigate any technical errors in the dialogue
  6. Test the integration in the builder if available

For full steps to follow, see Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents.

Common causes to a failed CRM connectivity

  • The bot doesn’t join conversations
  • Escalations to agents fail
  • Tagging errors occur during CRM-based flow handling

To resolve this issue:

  1. Verify whether the bot joins conversations
  2. Ensure that agent escalations and human hand offs succeed
  3. Verify if tags are applied correctly

For full steps to follow, see Troubleshooting CRM connection with AI agents.

When to contact Zendesk Customer Support about AI agents

Contact Zendesk Customer Support if:

  • The issue persists after you carefully verify your configuration against this article
  • The behavior is inconsistent across instances
  • You can replicate the issue
  • Logs show an error you can't resolve
  • The issue appears to be a product bug or backend provisioning problem

If you need help to submit a request, see How to contact Customer Support.

Before you contact Zendesk Customer Support

Gather the following details to assist with our investigation:

  • Your Zendesk subdomain or URL
  • The AI agent name
  • Channel
  • Conversation or ticket ID
  • Time of the issue
  • Steps to reproduce
  • Screenshots or screen recording
  • Relevant conversation logs
  • Any related integration details

Additional articles

  • Troubleshooting ticket automation in AI agents
  • Configuring escalation strategies and flows for AI agents
  • Configuring language detection for AI agents
  • Reviewing conversation logs for AI agents
  • Troubleshooting an integration for AI agents
  • Troubleshooting CRM connection with AI agents
  • Troubleshooting issues with web crawler imports for AI agents (AI agents - Advanced only)

articles.name: Announcing native call monitoring in Contact Center
articles.title: Announcing native call monitoring in Contact Center
articles.body:
We're excited to announce the availability of native call monitor and barge capabilities for Zendesk Contact Center. This new feature enables supervisors to listen to and participate in calls directly from their workflow, providing the same robust call management tools you enjoyed in the legacy Zendesk Contact Center app, but natively.
This announcement answers the following questions:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Monitor and barge give supervisors two distinct call management modes.

Silent Monitoring

From the Amazon Connect Agents dashboard, a supervisor can select a call to listen to. Clicking the button to monitor a call opens the relevant ticket in Zendesk with the call already in listen mode. The supervisor is not heard or seen by the other participants. Up to two supervisors can monitor a single call simultaneously, enabling team supervision and training.

Barge: Joining the call as an Active Participant

When intervention is needed, supervisors can switch from silent monitoring to joining the call. After joining, all other participants will be able to hear the supervisor. Only one supervisor can join the call at a time to avoid confusion. Once joined, supervisors have complete control over the call, including the ability to record or pause recordings, pause or mute other participants. However, supervisors cannot be muted, placed on hold, or disconnected by other participants, preserving their authority and preventing disruption.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Today's release is a major milestone on the road to making Zendesk Contact Center fully native. We're increasingly delivering feature parity compared with the marketplace app version while creating a much better, more integrated experience than before.

What do I need to do?

The feature will be available immediately after the rollout is complete.

For more information, see Configuring call monitoring in Contact Center and Monitoring and joining calls in Contact Center.

If you have feedback or questions about this announcement, visit our community forum, where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: What's new in Zendesk: July 2026
articles.title: What's new in Zendesk: July 2026
articles.body:
Click Follow in the What's New section to be notified each month when the What's New is published.

Check out what's new in the last month:

  • Support
  • Accounts and billing
  • AI agents
  • Copilot
  • Security
  • Knowledge
  • Messaging
  • Voice
  • Contact Center
  • Workforce management
  • Quality assurance
  • Apps and integrations

Also don't miss:

  • Zendesk early access programs
  • What's new videos on YouTube

Support

  • You can now select multiple custom roles and view their permissions side-by-side in a comparison matrix, making it easier to review role differences and update role settings without opening each role individually. See Comparing custom roles.
  • You can now filter tickets by the requester's email using "Is" and "Is not" operators, with email format validation to prevent errors. This enhances ticket organization and routing. See Creating views to build customized lists of tickets.
  • AI-based predictive routing in omnichannel routing uses AI to assign messaging tickets to the agents predicted to resolve them fastest, improving resolution times and agent efficiency. To help measure the success of predictive routing, a new metric called Agent Engagement Time tracks agent activity. See Using predictive routing in queues.
  • The new search side panel centralizes search tabs and saved searches from the top toolbar to a left-side panel, unifying the search experience and allowing for future expansion of search capabilities. See Searching Zendesk Support data.

  • Agents can now listen to audio messages attached to email comments directly within Zendesk without downloading files. This enhances support efficiency and accessibility. See Playing back audio comments in email and messaging tickets.

Accounts and billing

  • Digital customers with limited ARR can temporarily pause their accounts. As an alternative to cancelling your account, Zendesk allows eligible customers to pause their account for up to three months. See Pausing accounts.

AI agents

  • Forethought AI agents by Zendesk are now available for purchase as an add-on, offering autonomous customer inquiry resolution, intent identification, task automation, agent assistance, insights, and quality review. See About Forethought AI agents by Zendesk.
  • You can now directly edit generative procedure maps. Every time you create or edit a generative procedure, an AI-generated procedure map is produced. If you need to make targeted updates to the procedure map, you can directly edit it instead of relying on the AI generation. See Editing the procedure map.
  • Use cases can now be restricted so they're used only inside of dialogues. These use cases are triggered only inside dialogue flows and cannot be triggered during general conversation, such as when an AI agent is providing AI-generated answers. This helps ensure that use cases trigger only when they make sense. See Restricting a use case to be used only inside of a dialogue.

Copilot

  • The intelligent triage terminology update renames the "intent" classification to "topic" across the Copilot add-on. This is a naming change only with no impact on workflows or functionality.
  • Intelligent triage now includes guided onboarding to help you get started and create workflows based on your top customer trends. See About Intelligent triage.

Security

  • You can now use AI-generated, easy-to-understand descriptions for individual access log events, helping less-technical users quickly interpret complex log data and guiding investigations. See Using the access log to monitor agent activity.

Knowledge

  • You can now connect Google Drive as an external content source to make the content available for help center search, generative search, AI agents, and Agent Workspace. See Connecting Google Drive as an external knowledge source.
  • You can now connect a second Zendesk account to your primary account to access content from multiple Zendesk accounts in one place. This enhances content reuse across advanced AI agents, search, and generative search. See Connecting Zendesk as an external knowledge source.
  • You can now connect Amazon S3 as an external content source to make the content available for help center search, generative search, AI agents, and Agent Workspace. See Connecting Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source.
  • You can now connect Box as an external content source to make the content available for help center search, generative search, AI agents, and Agent Workspace. See Connecting Box as an external knowledge source.

Messaging

  • You can turn on end-user presence detection for messaging to identify if end users are still on your website or app or have abandoned the conversation, improving customer experience and reducing agent handle time. It uses presence signals to mark sessions as inactive when users leave, impacting queue and capacity management, and letting accounts automatically close or follow up on abandoned conversations. See Turning on end-user presence detection for messaging.
  • You can now use the behavioral customization APIs for Web Widget to programmatically control conversation flow and message handling in the Messaging Web Widget. This includes creating and fetching conversations, sending messages, navigating conversations, and intercepting messages. See Behavioral customization APIs for more details.
  • The Generated by AI disclosure redesign for social and mobile messaging channels integrates the "Generated by AI" label into the bot's display name for a cleaner conversational experience. It also introduces per-channel control via API to disable the AI label if desired. The redesigned label is applied automatically to mobile channels, and social channels need to confirm the update in their settings. To turn off the label per-channel, see the Update Integration API.

Voice

  • You can now set the number of times the IVR greeting repeats from one to three before routing to the default option, improving caller routing flexibility. The IVR settings page has also been refreshed with a modern look. See Routing incoming calls with IVR.
  • Admins can now restrict outbound calling by agent group in Voice (Talk). This lets you control which agent groups can use specific phone lines for outbound calls, helping reduce misrouted or costly calls. See Managing phone line settings.

Contact Center

  • The native Zendesk Contact Center is now generally available, integrated directly into the Zendesk Agent Workspace for a unified experience with single status management, call monitoring, simpler authentication, native settings, and expanded language support. See About Zendesk Contact Center.

Workforce management

  • WFM combined workstreams have been added to the Schedule page staffing panels. Combined workstreams now appear in Day view staffing panels, showing scheduled staffing without forecasting. This update helps users efficiently schedule and view staffing for combined workstreams. See Accessing staffing panels, menus, and charts.

  • Team visibility settings for time off reasons allow managers and admins to control which teams can see and use specific time off reasons, tailoring leave options to team-specific locations, perks, and policies. This change improves granularity by restricting time off reasons visibility to assigned teams only. See Creating and managing time off reasons for agents.

  • The Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM) experience is now available for Suite Professional plans and above at no extra cost. It provides visibility into agent activity and daily average handle time (AHT) for 24 hours, helping managers understand workload trends and improve planning. Agents can also see how much time they spend handling customer interactions. See Trying WFM for free using workforce activity tracking.

  • The out of adherence automation allows admins to configure automated real-time notifications that alert managers and agents the moment an agent is working on a task that doesn't match their current schedule. This change gives supervisors and managers the visibility they need to surface schedule deviations the moment they happen, enabling immediate course-correction rather than after-the-fact reporting. See About workforce management automations.

Quality assurance

  • The Coaching dashboard in Zendesk Quality Assurance (QA) provides an overview of coaching sessions, feedback reviews, and coaching impact on Internal Quality Score (IQS). It allows filtering by date, coach, and coachee, tracking session status, and reviewing coaching trends. See Accessing and viewing the Coaching dashboard in Zendesk QA.

Apps and integrations

  • The new action builder connectors introduce five new integrations—HubSpot, Chargebee, Stripe, Calendly, and GitHub—into Zendesk action flows, enabling automation of CRM, billing, scheduling, and engineering tasks directly within Zendesk. See Action builder and action flows for a full list of connectors.

articles.name: Accepting and declining calls in Contact Center
articles.title: Accepting and declining calls in Contact Center
articles.body:
This article describes how to accept and decline calls in Contact Center.

What's my plan?
Add-on Contact Center

Summary: ◀▼

Learn how to accept or decline Contact Center calls, set the right agent status, and use call details before answering. If you can’t take a call, decline it to send it to the next available agent and reduce customer wait time. You also need a supported browser and notifications turned on to get incoming call alerts.

This article describes how to accept and decline calls in Contact Center.

You must use a supported browser and, if you want your operating system to notify you of incoming calls, configure your browser for notifications.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Setting your status for calls
  • Accepting calls
  • Declining calls

Related articles:

  • Understanding how Contact Center calls become tickets

Setting your status for calls

To receive calls, you must set your status to a Zendesk status that maps to Available in Amazon Connect. The standard Zendesk status Transfer only isn't supported for Contact Center calls. For information about the Zendesk statuses and how to set your status, see Setting your agent status.

Tip: If you're an agent and don't know whether a given status maps to Available in Connect, ask your admin.

Accepting calls

To accept a call

  1. (Optional) Click Show details to view any associated information.

    The information comes from the contact flow and its contact attributes.
  2. Click Accept.

    When you accept the call, depending on your settings, Zendesk might begin recording the call and create a ticket. For details, see how calls become tickets. A counter shows the duration of the call.

    You can still show or hide the call details at this point.

  3. Click the close icon (X) to close the call notification and proceed with the call.

Declining calls

Calls are automatically routed to the first available agent. If that agent doesn't answer after a configurable amount of time, the call routes to the next available agent.

Don't wait for the allocated time to run out. Decline the call to send it straight to the next available agent so customers wait less time.

To decline a call

  1. (Optional) Click Show details to view any associated contact attributes.
  2. Click Decline to reject the call and put it back in the queue.

articles.name: Workflow: Choosing between client-side messaging metadata and AI agent conversation tags
articles.title: Workflow: Choosing between client-side messaging metadata and AI agent conversation tags
articles.body:
When working with messaging conversations, there are two different ways to use tags:

  1. Client-side messaging metadata, set by the Web Widget or SDKs
  2. Tags added in an AI agent flow, using Sunshine Conversations CRM actions such as Add conversation tags

These two methods can look similar because both can affect the context of a conversation and the ticket created from it. However, they are designed for different use cases and behave differently over time.

This article explains how each option works, when to use it, and what to consider when deciding which one is best for your workflow.

This workflow includes the following sections:

  • Understanding the difference
  • Tagging options for messaging conversations
  • Persistence of tags in Sunshine Conversations
  • When to use each tagging method
  • Additional resources

Understanding the difference

Client-side messaging metadata

Client-side messaging metadata is set from the frontend, such as:

  • the Web Widget loaded on a website
  • an SDK implementation

With this method, your developer sends metadata from the client into the messaging conversation. This can include conversation fields and conversation tags.

For tags, the Web Widget or SDK sends values using the messaging metadata APIs. In the conversation metadata, this appears as zen:ticket:tags.

This method is useful when the information is already available in the client, such as:

  • the product page the customer is viewing
  • an order number already displayed in the app
  • the customer’s current cart state
  • a known context that should be passed into the ticket

Adding tags to the conversation via a CRM action

This represents the CRM action in AI Advanced Agents that can be applied at different levels (see Creating and adding configured actions for AI agents).

This method is useful when the tag should be determined by the logic of the AI agent conversation itself, for example:

  • the user went through a specific path in a Dialogue or Procedure
  • the bot identified a use case during the conversation
  • the bot collected values that should be reflected in the conversation or downstream ticket

Although both methods use tags, they are not the same mechanism.

Tagging options for messaging conversations

Option 1: Using client-side messaging metadata

If you want to pass tags from the Web Widget or from a mobile SDK, use the client-side messaging metadata APIs.

This approach is useful when:

  • the tag value is known when the conversation begins
  • the tag comes from the page or app context
  • you want to pass context into the conversation before a ticket is created
  • you want to manage tag values from the frontend

Examples include:

  • tagging a conversation based on the page category
  • tagging a conversation with a product family
  • tagging a conversation based on the customer’s logged-in experience

Benefits of client-side metadata tags

Client-side metadata tags are a good fit when the tag is tied to the customer’s current session context in the widget or app.

Benefits include:

  • The data can be set automatically without asking the customer to provide it
  • The tags can be set as soon as the page or app state is known
  • The data can be cleared using the messaging metadata APIs when the frontend context changes
  • This approach is well suited to information that is only relevant to the current client-side experience

Option 2: Using tags in an AI agent flow

If you want the bot to decide when tags should be added during a conversation, use the Add conversation tags CRM action in your AI agent flow.

This approach is useful when:

  • the tag depends on what happens during the bot flow
  • the tag should be added after the user answers a question
  • the tag reflects the conversation path or intent
  • the tag is part of your AI agent logic rather than your frontend implementation

Examples include:

  • adding a tag after the customer selects a support topic
  • adding a tag when the AI agent identifies a billing or return scenario
  • adding a tag to influence downstream routing or reporting

Benefits of AI agent flow tags

Using Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow is a good fit when the tag should be controlled by the bot itself.

Benefits include:

  • Tags can be added based on conversation logic
  • Tags can use values collected during the flow
  • Tags can be applied consistently across channels where the AI agent is used
  • Tags can help downstream routing, reporting, or field selection logic

Persistence of tags in Sunshine Conversations

When using AI agent flow tagging with Sunshine Conversations, it is important to understand that information stored on a Sunshine Conversations conversation persists on that conversation.

In Sunshine Conversations, a conversation is a persistent object. Metadata and tags added to that conversation remain associated with it until they are changed or overwritten.

Because of this, Add conversation tags should be used with a clear understanding of how long the tag should remain attached to the conversation.

This leads to two valid implementation choices.

Choice 1: The tag should persist on the conversation

In some workflows, persistent tagging is the correct behavior.

Use this option when the tag is meant to remain associated with the conversation as part of the intended workflow, such as:

  • identifying the overall type of conversation
  • preserving a long-lived classification for future use
  • supporting downstream handling across the same Sunshine Conversations conversation

Choice 2: The tag is only valid for the current session

In other workflows, the tag is only relevant to the current session or a specific moment in the flow.

In this case, you should take steps to prevent the tag from carrying over unintentionally in the same Sunshine Conversations conversation.

A practical way to do this is to use the CRM action Overwrite conversation tags to clear the existing tags where needed.

For example, you can:

  • clear the conversation tags at the beginning of the flow
  • clear them at a specific point before applying new session-specific tags
  • clear them whenever you want to avoid carrying over metadata from an earlier interaction

If needed, an empty value can be submitted using a space character (" ") to effectively clear the existing conversation tags in the flow.

This approach is useful when tags are intended to be session-scoped rather than conversation-scoped.

When to use each tagging method

Client-side messaging metadata

Choose client-side messaging metadata when:

  • the tag value is known in the Web Widget or SDK
  • the information comes from the page or app context
  • the tag should be managed from the frontend
  • the context is tied to the current widget or app experience

Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow

Choose Add conversation tags in an AI agent flow when:

  • the bot should decide when to add the tag
  • the tag depends on user responses or flow logic
  • the tag is part of the AI agent workflow
  • persistent conversation-level tagging is acceptable or intended

If you use Add conversation tags for information that is only valid for the current session, plan how to clear or overwrite those tags so they do not carry over unexpectedly.

The most important distinction is behavior over time:

  • Client-side metadata is designed around frontend-managed context
  • Sunshine Conversations conversation tags persist on the conversation until they are changed or overwritten

Choosing the right method depends on whether the tag should reflect client context or bot flow logic, and whether the tag should be temporary or persistent.

Additional resources

If you would like to learn more, see:

  • Using messaging metadata with the Zendesk Web Widget and SDKs
  • About configured actions for AI agents
  • Creating and adding configured actions for AI agents
  • Available CRM actions for AI agents and Sunshine Conversations
  • Sunshine Conversations key concepts

articles.name: Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?
articles.title: Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?
articles.body:
Why does auto assist activity appear lower in Explore than expected?

Explore reports auto assist activity based on agent interaction with auto assist suggestions, not every time auto assist triggers or generates content.

A ticket is included in the current auto assist reporting only when an agent interacts with a suggestion, for example, when an agent performs one of these tasks:

  • Accepts it
  • Edits it
  • Dismisses it

If auto assist generates a suggestion but the agent does not interact with it, that activity is not included in the available Copilot auto assist dataset.

This also means some tickets do not appear in reporting even though auto assist was active on the ticket, for example:

  • A suggestion was still being generated and auto assist was turned off before the suggestion was available
  • A suggestion was generated, but it did not contain any actionable content for the agent to interact with
  • A suggestion was generated, but the agent did not use or respond to it

Because of this, auto assist metrics in Explore can be lower than counts from ticket searches or other reports with the agent_copilot_enabled ticket tag. Those sources can show where auto assist is turned on, triggered, or generated a suggestion, while Explore reflects only suggestions that result in recorded agent interaction.

The current auto assist reporting is a measure of agent interaction with auto assist suggestions, rather than a complete count of every auto assist trigger, generated suggestion, or ticket where a procedure applies.

articles.name: Announcing a new minimum Android version requirement for the Zendesk messaging SDK
articles.title: Announcing a new minimum Android version requirement for the Zendesk messaging SDK
articles.body:
We’re updating the minimum Android version requirement for the Zendesk messaging SDK for Android from API 21 (Android 5.0, Lollipop) to API 24 (Android 7.0, Nougat). This change allows us to deliver a more stable, performant, and feature-rich SDK experience by taking full advantage of modern Android platform capabilities. This update is taking place on July 21, 2026.

This article includes the following sections:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Starting with the next major release of the Zendesk messaging SDK for Android, API 24 (Android 7.0, Nougat) will be the minimum supported version. Devices running Android versions below API 24 will no longer be supported.

If your app currently sets a minSdkVersion below 24, you will need to raise it to continue receiving SDK updates.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

The vast majority of end users on the Zendesk messaging SDK are already running Android 7.0 or later. Raising the minimum version allows us to:

  • Stay current with Jetpack Compose — Starting with Compose BOM 2025.12.00, the minimum required API level is 23. Remaining on API 21 creates a risk of runtime crashes when customers update their own Compose BOM version, as incompatibilities between Compose versions are not always caught at compile time. Raising to API 24 keeps us current and removes this fragility.
  • Align with AndroidX and modern Jetpack libraries — AndroidX’s default minimum SDK is reviewed annually and is currently API 23. Future library updates are likely to require higher API levels, creating the same risks we currently face with Compose. Raising our minimum to API 24 provides a buffer above the current AndroidX default and positions us ahead of the next review cycle.
  • Improve security and compliance — API 24 introduces the Network Security Config, hardware-backed key attestation, and a stricter certificate trust model that no longer trusts user-added CAs by default. It also removes the need for the WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE dangerous permission on lower API levels, reducing exposure to Google Play policy warnings for integrators.
  • Simplify the SDK codebase — Supporting older API levels requires conditional code paths and legacy compatibility shims that increase maintenance complexity and regression risk. Raising the minimum to API 24 allows us to remove this legacy code, resulting in a cleaner and more maintainable SDK.
  • Align with industry trends and usage data — Only 0.008% of over 620 million SDK hits analyzed come from devices below API 24. The vast majority of top customers’ apps already target API 26 or higher. Competitors including Intercom and Airship have already adopted API 23 as their minimum, with Intercom announcing a move to API 23 in their next release.

What do I need to do?

If your app already targets API 24 (Android 7.0) or later, no action is required. You will continue to receive SDK updates as normal.

If your app currently targets below API 24, you will need to raise your app’s minSdkVersion to 24 before upgrading to the new SDK version. Apps that do not update their minSdkVersion will continue to work on the last SDK version that supported API 21, but will not receive new features or fixes.

To update your minimum SDK version in Android Studio:

  1. Open your app-level build.gradle file in Android Studio.
  2. Locate the defaultConfig block.

  3. Set 

    <>minSdkVersion to 24.
  4. Sync your project with Gradle files.

For more information about integrating and configuring the Zendesk messaging SDK for Android, see Zendesk messaging SDK for Android.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Announcing view conditions for requester email address
articles.title: Announcing view conditions for requester email address
articles.body:
We’re excited to announce a new condition to Zendesk views under requester fields, that lets you filter tickets by the requester’s email address.

This article includes the following sections:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Previously, views did not support filtering directly by requester email. With this update, you can now create views using the Requester’s email address with the following operators:

  • Is
  • Is not

To help prevent an invalid configuration, Zendesk will validate the email address format before a view can be saved.

This makes it easier to create precise views that target tickets based on requester email criteria.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Customers need a more flexible and accurate way to organize and monitor tickets in views. Filtering by requester email address helps teams quickly surface tickets from specific users, exclude certain requesters, and better manage workflows.

This enhancement improves ticket routing and visibility while keeping existing views functionality unchanged.

What do I need to do?

No action is required. This feature will be rolled out to all accounts over the next week. Once available in your account, you'll automatically see the updated verification icons in your Customer List

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Announcing new action builder connectors
articles.title: Announcing new action builder connectors
articles.body:
We're excited to announce five new connectors for action flows, bringing essential business automation capabilities to your Zendesk workflows: HubSpot, Chargebee, Stripe, Calendly, and GitHub.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What's changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What's changing?

New connectors bring customer lifecycle automation and cross-team collaboration directly into Zendesk action flows: 

  • With HubSpot, teams can create and update contacts, companies, deals, and tickets to keep CRM records in sync. 
  • Chargebee supports subscription actions like provisioning, billing changes, pauses, cancellations, and credit notes. 
  • Stripe enables refunds, invoice creation, customer payment searches, and other billing tasks without leaving Zendesk. 
  • Calendly simplifies scheduling with booking links, invitations, and event searches.
  • GitHub helps support teams escalate bugs and feature requests, create and update pull requests, and track engineering progress—all from one place.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Support teams often work across CRM, billing, scheduling, and engineering tools to manage customer requests, but manually updating records, handling billing changes, coordinating meetings, and escalating bugs can slow resolution and create gaps in context. 

These new connectors bring customer lifecycle automation, scheduling, and engineering collaboration directly into action flows, enabling teams to sync CRM data, manage subscriptions and payments, automate bookings, and route product feedback or issues without leaving Zendesk.

What do I need to do?

No action is required. These connectors are being rolled out to all accounts with access to action flows. 

To learn more about each connector and how to connect them to action builder, see:

  • Using Calendly actions in action flows
  • Using Chargebee actions in action flows
  • Using HubSpot actions in action flows
  • Using Stripe actions in action flows
  • Using GitHub actions in action flows

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Error: "This email is already taken. Try another email"
articles.title: Error: "This email is already taken. Try another email"
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When you try adding an email address to your Support addresses, or bulk update users with a comma-separated values (CSV) file, you receive an error or an email with a link to download an Excel file: Email: User couldn't be updated - This email is already taken. Try another email.

Resolving the "This email is already taken. Try another email" error

This error means you are trying to use an email address already used by another user on your account. That account may have been created by an email forwarded to your Support address.

To resolve the This email is already taken error:

  1. First, verify if the email address is a Support address, then remove it
  2. If no Support address exists, search Zendesk Support with the email address from the error and confirm which user account already is associated with that email address
  3. If you find a duplicate, open the user profile to either delete the user, or replace the email address with a fictional one and verify that email address to validate the change
  4. A final option if you have multiple users is to merge your users

For more information, see this article: Bulk importing users.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do I receive the error "This email is already taken. Try another email"? What does the error "Email: User couldn't be updated - This email is already taken. Try another email" mean? Why can I not add an email address or update users?

This error means another user or customer account on your Zendesk Support help desk already uses that email address. An email forwarded to your Support address sometimes creates that duplicate account.

How do I resolve the "This email is already taken. Try another email" error? How do I fix the user couldn't be updated error when bulk updating users or adding a Support address? How can I repair duplicate email address conflicts?

  1. Verify if the email address is a Support address, then remove it.
  2. If no Support address exists, search Zendesk Support or your ticketing system with the email address from the error and confirm which user or customer account already is associated with that email address.
  3. If you find a duplicate, open the user profile to either delete the user, or replace the email address with a fictional one and verify that email address to validate the change.
  4. Merge your users or team members if you have multiple users.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 19, 2026 - AI Agents | OpenAI service delays and error issues
articles.title: Service Incident - June 19, 2026 - AI Agents | OpenAI service delays and error issues
articles.body:
Summary

On June 19, 2026, between 14:19 UTC and 15:01 UTC, AI Agents customers across all pods experienced service delays and errors when using AI features such as composing and enhancing messages, due to an issue affecting the Open AI service in the GCP EU region.

Timeline

June 19, 2026 16: UTC | June 19, 2026 9:30 AM PT

We are aware of an issue that affected AI Agents and Zendesk Support AI features between 14:09 and 15:01 UTC on June 19. The incident was caused by a service degradation with our vendor, OpenAI, resulting in slow responses or intermittent errors for some customers. This issue has been fully resolved, and all services are operating normally. We apologize for the inconvenience caused.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by an upstream service disruption in the EU region that impacted the flow of data through our system, resulting in cascading failures that affected AI agent performance.

Resolution

The issue was resolved by monitoring until the external service stabilized and recovered, after which related systems resumed normal operation without the need for manual intervention.

Remediation Items

  1. Explore options for automatic failover with the API provider to minimize service disruptions.
  2. Investigate why the on-call team was not contacted by phone during this incident and improve notification processes.
  3. Replace the global fallback setting with service-specific fallback configurations for better reliability.
  4. Confirm whether the fallback option for the Azure OpenAI service is properly configured.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: [Updated date and time] Scheduled Maintenance -  July 8, 2026 | All Pods - Copilot Features
articles.title: [Updated date and time] Scheduled Maintenance -  July 8, 2026 | All Pods - Copilot Features
articles.body:
Zendesk will perform critical maintenance impacting Copilot features for customers on all Pods on July 8, 2026. Maintenance will occur from 8am UTC to 10pm UTC, with the maintenance for each account lasting up to 15 minutes. We are upgrading our database for improved performance and ongoing reliability.

Affected products: Copilot features

Important:  Only customers using Copilot features will be impacted.

Customers Affected: Zendesk customers on all Pods will experience a brief service disruption of up to 15 minutes at some time within the 8am UTC to 10pm UTC maintenance window.

 

Expected behavior: The database upgrade may cause noticeable delays in features such as Auto Assist, Intelligent Triage, and other Copilot features.

What do customers have to do? No action is required. Features will return to normal service following the maintenance.

Why we're doing this: We are increasing database size to provide additional performance and ensure stability of our systems going forward.

articles.name: Announcing Amazon S3 as an external content source for use across knowledge experiences
articles.title: Announcing Amazon S3 as an external content source for use across knowledge experiences
articles.body:
We’re excited to announce the general availability of Amazon S3 as a knowledge source. You can now connect Amazon S3 to your Zendesk account to make the content available wherever external content is used.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

You can now connect Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source to your Zendesk account to make the content available wherever external content is used. This expands the content sources available, enabling access to your existing Amazon S3 content across your knowledge experiences.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

We’re making it easier to reuse existing knowledge from different sources your organization already uses and leverage it within Zendesk. So now, your AI agents, search, and generative search can tap into your Amazon S3 knowledge.

What do I need to do?

You can create an Amazon S3 connection on any Zendesk Suite or Knowledge plan. For more information, see the following resources:

  • Connecting Amazon S3 as an external knowledge source
  • Connecting knowledge sources to power generative replies in agents

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-06-19
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-06-19
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

  • App Marketplace

Voice

New:

Fixed:

App Marketplace

New:

XCHK Verification lets Zendesk agents launch a live video verification session directly from a ticket sidebar. The app verifies the requester's real-time location against their expected location and checks device authenticity, including detection of browser language/timezone spoofing and VPN/proxy use. After the session, PASS or FAIL results are posted back to the Zendesk ticket as an internal note with tags, and can trigger Zendesk automations. When Zendesk OAuth is connected in XCHK, results sync automatically back to the ticket.

Call and SMS activity is matched to tickets under a customer profile, so you can track the entire ticket history in Zendesk.
